evidence_quotes.quote: a purchase and not just a temporary license

paragraphs.text: For the term digital fairness to carry any weight it must include protections for consumers in this trying time of corporations pretty much doing whatever they damn please. There are so many things to mention here that i don't even know where to start. Though what brought me here is the stop killing games initiative. This might not seem a natural place to start talking about fairness in todays world, but this is one of the first places many of the new citizens of Europe might encounter the negative side of the very one-sided consumerism that permeats the digital world. A world where it is very easy to feel helpless in the face of digital mastodonts dictating what your rights are. Having a piece of your digital identity taken away with no recourse can be brutal. Ensuring that your purchase of a computer game is actually a purchase and not just a temporary license, even though some tiny point in a disclaimer to long to read says so, is an important step forward in restoring faith in the economy that so many today feel is just something to abide by as so many of the brutal king's of yesteryears whims. Please make sure that this is a part of the digital fairness act.
